steps.title: Identify what insufficient data leaves
steps.content: The key signal is “Earth-based observations had yielded very little information.” Without solid observational evidence, astronomers' pre-mission ideas could only be guesses, not firm knowledge.

steps.title: Match the word for educated guessing
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean uncertain guesses — astronomers had almost no observational data about Venus before Mariner 2, so their ideas could not be firmly grounded. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Summations: ideas were "little more than summations" — summations means totals or summaries compiled from evidence, implying data that doesn't exist here. ✗
  • Choice B — Conjectures: ideas were "little more than conjectures" — conjectures means educated guesses formed without solid evidence, which fits: with very little observational data, astronomers could only speculate. ✓
  • Choice C — Exemplifications: ideas were "little more than exemplifications" — exemplifications means illustrations of a principle using examples, implying established knowledge to illustrate. ✗
  • Choice D — Conclusions: ideas were "little more than conclusions" — conclusions are findings drawn from evidence; scarce data produces guesses, not conclusions. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
